qa_pairs.question: What strategies should users employ when migrating data in hundreds of gigabyte blocks without using Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: When migrating data in blocks of a few hundred gigabytes at a time without Globus, users should make a schedule to migrate their data. If a transfer stops, they should be able to resume from the incomplete file, avoiding the need to re-transfer files that are already complete. An organized list of files will assist with this process.

qa_pairs.question: How can users monitor the progress of a non-Globus data transfer and what action should they take if it appears to have stopped?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should regularly check that the transfer process has not stopped, using file size as a good indicator of progress. If no files have changed size for several minutes, something may have gone wrong. If restarting the transfer does not work, users should contact technical support.
